title: The Rollout

jokes.text: Um, who taught you rollout? - May have been me. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Johnny keeps saying 'rollout' incorrectly in business context
jokes.punchline: David's casual admission he taught his father the wrong term
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David's casual responsibility for his father's confusion, delivered with trademark deadpan

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I may have taught you that word incorrectly.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yeah, I've known for a while, but I thought it was funny.

jokes.text: Okay, you really need to stop saying rollout.
jokes.setup: Johnny has been repeatedly saying 'rollout' throughout the scene
jokes.punchline: David finally breaks and directly asks him to stop
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag, David's exasperation feels earned

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, please stop saying 'rollout.'

jokes.text: Well I can't pull the account, because it's part of the rollout.
jokes.setup: David just told him to stop saying rollout
jokes.punchline: Johnny immediately says it again, obliviously
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of running gag - Johnny's complete obliviousness to David's frustration

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What did I just say about the rollout?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, stop saying rollout.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, we need to discuss the rollout.

jokes.text: So, I may be looking elsewhere when it comes time for the pop-out store.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to use business terminology correctly
jokes.punchline: Gets another term wrong - 'pop-out' instead of 'pop-up'
jokes.explanation: Extends the pattern of Johnny's business terminology failures, character-specific humor

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We need to think outside the box here. I'm talking about a pop-out store.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A pop-out store?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, you know, where the store just... pops out.

jokes.text: Okay. He meant pop-up store, right? Honestly, David, it took him like 2 weeks to learn rollout, so...
jokes.setup: Johnny just said 'pop-out store'
jokes.punchline: Stevie's matter-of-fact explanation of Johnny's learning curve
jokes.explanation: Stevie's deadpan delivery and the absurd timeline of learning basic business terms is perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: He doesn't know what a pop-up store is. He thinks it's a store that pops out of the ground.

jokes.text: it's kinda hard to guarantee fun.
jokes.setup: Alexis wants happy, fun, flirty puppies for Singles Week
jokes.punchline: Ted's dry observation about rescue dogs
jokes.explanation: Ted's practical veterinarian perspective punctures Alexis's event planning fantasy perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Most of the dogs at the shelter are, um, rescue dogs. So they've been through some trauma. They're not really in a 'flirty' phase right now.

jokes.text: That's actually a he, and I think he might already be spoken for?
jokes.setup: Alexis calling a male dog 'she' and getting excited about him
jokes.punchline: Ted's gentle correction on both the gender and availability
jokes.explanation: Small character moment showing Ted's patience and Alexis's assumptions

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, she's so cute!

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Actually, he's a he. And he's married.

jokes.text: Hmm. Wow, love that for her.
jokes.setup: Ted explains Heather wants to adopt the dog Alexis liked
jokes.punchline: Alexis's passive-aggressive response disguised as positivity
jokes.explanation: Peak Alexis - fake enthusiasm masking disappointment, perfectly delivered passive aggression

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's so great! I'm really happy for Heather. She's going to be such a good dog mom. Like, honestly, I think she's probably better suited for this than I am anyway. I mean, I'm just so busy all the time, and she's just got so much more love to give, you know? So yeah, no, this is perfect. I'm genuinely thrilled for her.

jokes.text: Oh, Theodore, back for another meeting of the animals, I see.
jokes.setup: Ted arriving at animal adoption meeting
jokes.punchline: Moira's pretentious way of referring to the puppy adoption event
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira pomposity - turning a simple puppy meeting into something that sounds like a nature documentary

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm hosting a soirée for the rescue puppy initiative. It's a very exclusive gathering of the town's most discerning pet enthusiasts.

jokes.text: You know you two are lovely little freaks. In this digital day of discontented disconnection, you two still manage to do things face-to-face, like people from before your time.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Ted and Alexis meeting in person
jokes.punchline: Moira's over-the-top analysis of basic human interaction
jokes.explanation: Pure Moira - turning normal behavior into pretentious sociological observation with her signature vocabulary

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, they made eye contact. That's either the beginning of a beautiful romance or the prelude to a violent crime. There's really no in-between.

jokes.text: Safe to say our animal-loving singles will be well placated.
jokes.setup: Finishing the conversation about puppy adoption
jokes.punchline: Moira's unnecessarily formal way to say people will be happy
jokes.explanation: More Moira vocabulary - 'placated' is hilariously clinical for describing happy singles

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: People will experience a profound sense of joy and fulfillment in their domestic arrangements.

jokes.text: That nudnick at the curling rink just informed me that they'll no longer be able to accommodate our Lover's Messy Sloppy Joe Eat-a-Thon.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining a venue cancellation
jokes.punchline: The absurd name of the event delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: The event name is brilliantly ridiculous - combining romance with messy eating, delivered with Moira's theatrical gravity

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The venue called to cancel. Apparently, there was a scheduling conflict with their annual Squirt and Twirl.

jokes.text: N... alas! Your co-chair will be busy getting herself out of jury duty today.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking why Moira can't find a new venue
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic excuse about jury duty with theatrical 'alas'
jokes.explanation: Moira's dramatic delivery of 'alas' for something as mundane as jury duty is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alas, I cannot. I've been summoned for jury duty.

jokes.text: Alexis, you know me, I'm not one to overstep, But, perhaps you might be able to strike a few more to-do's off that list if you and Ted spend a little less time fussing over this canine auction.
jokes.setup: Moira claiming she doesn't overstep
jokes.punchline: Immediately overstepping by critiquing Alexis and Ted's work
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira contradiction - claiming not to overstep while actively doing exactly that

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I would never overstep. I respect boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That said, Alexis, your presentation was rather flat, and Ted, your tie choice was actively working against you.

jokes.text: It's a puppy adoption, and we're done. Planning. So, thank you.
jokes.setup: Moira calling it a 'canine auction' and criticizing their time
jokes.punchline: Alexis's irritated correction and dismissal with awkward pauses
jokes.explanation: Alexis's frustration breaking through her usual composure, the pauses make it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's not a canine auction, Mother. It's a dog show. And we're not spending the whole day there.

jokes.text: Uh I know it's for the guests, but it's like really good stuff, and this is extra.
jokes.setup: Stevie has a rash and David catches her using guest lotion
jokes.punchline: Her guilty admission while actively making the rash worse
jokes.explanation: Stevie's rationalization for using the lotion that's causing her rash is perfectly illogical

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Is that my lotion?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're using my lotion.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I have a rash.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's a guest lotion. You can't use guest lotion on a rash.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, it's too late now.

jokes.text: Nothing, just rolling things out, like you asked me to.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks what she's been doing differently
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan use of 'rolling out' - the cursed phrase
jokes.explanation: Great callback - the business speak has infected everyone, delivered perfectly deadpan

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What have you been doing differently?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I've just been rolling out new ideas for the business.

jokes.text: I don't know, okay! It might be causing the rash, but it's also the only thing that's making it feel better.
jokes.setup: David asking why she keeps using the lotion
jokes.punchline: Stevie's completely contradictory logic delivered with frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd logic - the thing causing the problem is also the solution, delivered with Stevie's trademark exasperation

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Because my skin is dry. But also because I don't want to use too much of it, so I'm rationing it. But then my skin gets drier, so I use more. It's a vicious cycle, David, and frankly, I don't appreciate the interrogation.

jokes.text: This is the last thing we need in the middle of a rollout!
jokes.setup: The ongoing problems with the rash situation
jokes.punchline: Johnny still saying 'rollout' despite earlier complaints
jokes.explanation: Continues the rollout running gag - Johnny is completely committed to this word despite the criticism

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We need to do a rollout of the new product line.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Johnny, didn't we just discuss this? The rash—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A full rollout. Nationwide.

jokes.text: I, of course, am your co-chair, Alexis Rose.
jokes.setup: Alexis introducing herself to volunteers
jokes.punchline: The unnecessary self-aggrandizing 'of course'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's self-importance

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Alexis Rose, of course.

jokes.text: Oh well, I uh, I don't know if you heard, but I'm gonna have a little brother soon, so yeah, I couldn't miss that.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking why he's back from his trip
jokes.punchline: Mutt's casual way of announcing his father is having a baby
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward family situation delivered with Mutt's characteristic understatement

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Why are you back? I thought you were going to be gone for another week.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, my dad's having a baby, so I figured I'd come back.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: My stepmom is pregnant.

jokes.text: It didn't slip my mind, I just like, didn't go.
jokes.setup: Mutt saying his friend was supposed to watch the barn
jokes.punchline: Alexis's honest admission of complete negligence
jokes.explanation: Peak Alexis honesty - no excuse, just direct admission of not caring enough to help

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I completely forgot about the barn. I didn't even know we had a barn.

jokes.text: Yeah, a nice little home for a family of raccoons. I had to evict them, though, and they didn't go quietly.
jokes.setup: Explaining the state of the barn
jokes.punchline: Describing raccoon removal like a landlord-tenant dispute
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor treating raccoons like difficult tenants - 'they didn't go quietly' is perfectly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah, so the raccoons have been here for about three years now. I've tried everything to get them out, but they've basically established squatter's rights at this point. I even left them an eviction notice, but they can't read, so that didn't really work.

jokes.text: I won't turn down the help, but you don't have to worry about the plants, they are very, very dead.
jokes.setup: Alexis offering to help water the plants
jokes.punchline: Mutt's matter-of-fact announcement about plant mortality
jokes.explanation: The emphasis on 'very, very dead' sells the complete neglect perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I can help you water the plants.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: They're all dead.

jokes.text: Mutt, you're making me feel partially responsible.
jokes.setup: Mutt explaining all the damage from her not watching the barn
jokes.punchline: Alexis's understated admission of obvious guilt
jokes.explanation: 'Partially responsible' is hilariously understated given she was completely responsible

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I may have let a few things slide.

jokes.text: Oh no, I've been waiting for this moment. Apparently, if they call your number, you get to stand in front of a judge! I feel like I'm on 'The Voice'!
jokes.setup: Moira expressing sympathy about jury duty
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's excitement about jury duty like it's a game show
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jocelyn's enthusiasm for completely misunderstood situations - jury duty as entertainment

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Jury duty sounds absolutely exhausting. You have my deepest sympathies.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, it's going to be amazing! I might get sequestered! Do you know what that means? It means I get to stay in a hotel for FREE!

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: And I get to decide someone's fate. It's like being on a game show, except instead of winning a toaster, I'm determining guilt or innocence!

jokes.text: Jocelyn, you're about to witness a master class in judicatory persuasion.
jokes.setup: Moira preparing to get out of jury duty
jokes.punchline: Her pretentious way of describing jury manipulation
jokes.explanation: 'Judicatory persuasion' is peak Moira - making jury duty sound like academic theater

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm going to tell them I'm clairvoyant. That way, they'll have to dismiss me because I'll know what the verdict is before the trial even begins.

jokes.text: No one at the counter! Who runs a business like this? Anyone could just walk in here and shoplift.
jokes.setup: Johnny entering David's store
jokes.punchline: Complaining about security while literally walking in unattended
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony - Johnny proves his own point about store security

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, your security is absolutely abysmal. I walked right in here without anyone stopping me. There could be anyone wandering around this store.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, you own the store.

jokes.text: I look like the Phantom of the Opera!
jokes.setup: David has a rash on his face
jokes.punchline: His dramatic comparison to a classic theater villain
jokes.explanation: Perfect David dramatics - turning a rash into theater tragedy

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I look like Phantom of the Opera if he gave up and let the mask win.

jokes.text: You realize you're gonna have to talk to Patrick and tell him that you two are gonna have to take a bit of a breather when it... comes to... - Ew! - You know, could be contagious.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting David might be contagious
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted reaction to his father discussing his relationship
jokes.explanation: Classic father-son awkwardness - Johnny's well-meaning but inappropriate relationship advice

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You might be contagious.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not contagious, Dad.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, you've been looking pretty rough lately. When's the last time you got laid?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not discussing my sex life with you.

jokes.text: And here you were this morning, acting like you were doing us a favour with the rollout.
jokes.setup: The rash situation making David's products look bad
jokes.punchline: Johnny using 'rollout' again while criticizing David
jokes.explanation: Continues the rollout obsession while adding dramatic irony about the product failure

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, your skincare line is causing people to break out in a rash. We need to halt the rollout immediately.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, it's not the products. The customers aren't following the instructions.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, the only thing rolling out right now is hives on people's faces. Cancel the rollout.

jokes.text: Well, we're gonna have to roll it back in.
jokes.setup: Deciding to recall the problematic products
jokes.punchline: Johnny's 'roll it back' continuing the rollout theme
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the rollout gag - Johnny finds a business-appropriate way to keep using roll terminology

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We need to recall these products immediately. Roll it back.

jokes.text: I totally thought she was like, the one for you.
jokes.setup: Mutt explaining his trip ended badly
jokes.punchline: Alexis's oblivious romantic assessment of his failed relationship
jokes.explanation: Alexis missing social cues and making awkward romantic commentary

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah, it didn't work out. She wanted different things.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's so sad. But you know what? That just means she's not your soulmate. Your soulmate is still out there waiting for you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And honestly? That's actually really romantic.

jokes.text: She's married to Moonshine now, the owner of the cone farm.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened with his travel companion
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'Moonshine' and concept of a cone farm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mutt-world absurdity - casual mention of people with names like Moonshine running cone farms

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: So my travel companion was this guy named Moonshine, and we were going to start a cone farm together.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A cone farm?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah, you know, growing pine cones and selling them to craft stores. It seemed like a really solid business plan at the time.

jokes.text: Compass broke halfway there, we ended up following a star for 2 days.
jokes.setup: Explaining how their trip went wrong
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of using celestial navigation like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - Mutt's matter-of-fact delivery of following a star like Biblical wise men is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah, so we got a bit lost on the way back. GPS wasn't working, so we just used the stars to navigate home.

jokes.text: That day I picked 700 cones! Well, Moonshine and his daughter, Petal, said it was the biggest one-day haul they'd ever seen. They gave me the Cone of Achievement, which allowed me to take 2 showers that week.
jokes.setup: Mutt describing his cone-picking experience
jokes.punchline: The absurd reward system and hygiene rationing
jokes.explanation: Peak Mutt absurdity - treating cone picking like an Olympic sport with formal awards and shower rationing

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah, so we'd pick cones all day, and at the end of the week, if you picked the most cones, you'd get a coupon for a free soft-serve ice cream.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: But here's the thing - they rationed how often you could shower. So if you won the ice cream, you had to choose between eating it and using your weekly shower allotment to wash off the cone sap.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Most guys just chose to stay sticky.

jokes.text: I can crack open a bottle of cone wine? Feeling risky?
jokes.setup: Offering Alexis a drink while cleaning
jokes.punchline: Cone wine as a real beverage option
jokes.explanation: Extends the cone farm absurdity - of course they make wine from cones, delivered as tempting offer

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Can I get you something to drink? We have water, coffee, or cone wine.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Cone wine?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: It's wine served in a cone. Like an ice cream cone, but wine.

jokes.text: I gotta tell you two, this timesheet you have is really complicated. By the time I finished filling it out, I was already into overtime. So do you guys have an overtime sheet I can fill out?
jokes.setup: Roland talking about timesheets at the motel
jokes.punchline: Creating more work by documenting the work of documenting work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic absurdism - the recursive nature of paperwork creating more paperwork. Classic Roland obtuseness.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Creating more work by documenting the work of documenting work

jokes.text: Well, boy, leave it to Stevie to get a rash from a moisturizer when she's sitting right next to poison oak.
jokes.setup: Everyone blaming David's moisturizer for the rash
jokes.punchline: Roland casually identifying the real culprit
jokes.explanation: Great plot twist delivered casually - reveals the real cause after extended misdirection

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I hate to break it to you, but that rash ain't from no moisturizer. That's from the poison ivy I've been using to edge the garden beds.

jokes.text: Uh, correct me if I'm wrong here, Johnny, but I don't think poison oak is that expensive.
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions choosing more economical flowers
jokes.punchline: Roland's dry observation about the cost of weeds
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan highlighting the absurdity of Johnny's cost-saving backfiring

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, weeds are free, so there's your economical option right there.

jokes.text: Well, there was that summer that Jimmy Smits stole my heart on stage in a workshop-only production of 'An Officer and a Gentleman'. I suppose that might be called emotional embezzlement?
jokes.setup: Judge asking about experiences similar to embezzlement case
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic interpretation involving celebrity crushes
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - turning legal proceedings into theater anecdotes with 'emotional embezzlement' concept

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I have had experiences where I've been completely infatuated with someone, only to discover that they've been taking me for granted. I'm thinking of Brad Pitt circa 1995.

jokes.text: The fact that my own world was ripped out from under me, by someone like this prick of a prestidigitator? I should think would help the court in the quest to make a fair judgment.
jokes.setup: Moira supposedly trying to be fair about the embezzlement case
jokes.punchline: Her obvious bias revealed with 'prick of a prestidigitator'
jokes.explanation: Moira's alliterative insult 'prick of a prestidigitator' while claiming impartiality is perfectly hypocritical

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm trying to be very fair about this embezzlement case. I have no preconceived notions, and I'm certainly not biased against the defendant.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Although, I must say, he does strike me as a prick of a prestidigitator.

jokes.text: Mother (bleep). This is the farthest I've ever gone!
jokes.setup: Both Moira and Jocelyn getting dismissed from jury duty
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's disappointment at her 'personal best' in jury selection
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn treating jury duty like a personal achievement system is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that was humiliating. I've never been dismissed from a jury before.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, I have. Multiple times. I thought I was doing so well this time. I really had a good feeling about it.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: The judge asked me if I could be impartial, and I said yes, but apparently my face said no. This was going to be my personal best.

jokes.text: Mother (bleep). This is the farthest I've ever gone!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn gets dismissed from jury selection
jokes.punchline: Her swearing and treating dismissal like a personal achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jocelyn moment - finding victory in what should be disappointment

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Well, I guess that's it for me. I'm off the jury. Finally! Do you know how long I've been waiting for this moment? This is the best day of my life!

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I mean, who needs to serve on a jury when you can just go home and watch TV? Thank God they realized I'm too fabulous for this courtroom.

jokes.text: Makes you wonder if it's us?
jokes.setup: Discussing how they're organizing Singles Week but both still single
jokes.punchline: Mutt's philosophical observation about their own relationship status
jokes.explanation: Mutt's self-aware moment about irony of organizing dating events while being single

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, we're organizing Singles Week and we're both still single, so I guess we're just two single people throwing a party for single people.

jokes.text: Okay, bye.
jokes.setup: After Mutt's philosophical moment about relationships
jokes.punchline: Alexis's abrupt exit without explanation
jokes.explanation: The abruptness after their moment shows Alexis realizing she needs to act on her feelings

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm going to go... somewhere else now.

jokes.text: My skin is normally fresh, and dewy, and blemish-free.
jokes.setup: David explaining his skin condition to the vendor
jokes.punchline: His vain description during a crisis call
jokes.explanation: David's vanity overtaking the practical problem - perfect character moment

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, hello, I'm calling because I have a serious skin condition and I need to speak to someone immediately. It's very urgent. No, I don't have time to wait on hold. Yes, I understand you have other patients, but this is a dermatological emergency. What do you mean 'how bad is it'? Well, my complexion has taken on a rather sallow, almost gray undertone, and there's a slight dryness around my T-zone that's absolutely unacceptable. My moisturizer isn't absorbing properly and I can see the beginning of what might be a fine line—possibly two fine lines—near my left eye. It's catastrophic.

jokes.text: Well, does the vendor have a sense of humor, because this is a good one!
jokes.setup: David is stressed about vendor relations
jokes.punchline: Johnny's misplaced enthusiasm about the revelation being 'funny'
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Johnny's obliviousness to consequences

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, this isn't funny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think it's hilarious.

jokes.text: Remember the conversation we were having this morning about you know, the rash? Well, it turns out it wasn't the moisturizer, it was poison oak.
jokes.setup: Johnny's casual delivery of major news
jokes.punchline: The complete reversal of who's responsible for the problem
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - Johnny casually revealing he caused the entire crisis

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I have something to tell you. We've lost everything.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? How is that possible?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, it turns out I'm not responsible for this one. Your mother is.

jokes.text: I'm sorry for touching my nose to a flower!
jokes.setup: David learning the poison oak came from Johnny's flowers
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic defense of normal flower-smelling behavior
jokes.explanation: David's indignant defense of basic flower appreciation is perfectly delivered sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't realize that a normal, healthy person isn't supposed to smell flowers. What was I thinking? Next you'll tell me I shouldn't be touching things or breathing air.

jokes.text: I just went all Gordon Ramsey on one of my most important vendors! I basically told her, her product was like a jar of Ebola!
jokes.setup: David realizing he wrongly blamed the vendor
jokes.punchline: His dramatic description of his phone call
jokes.explanation: Gordon Ramsay reference and 'jar of Ebola' perfectly capture David's dramatic business panic

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I may have been slightly harsh with the vendor.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I called him and I said, 'Your flowers are garbage, your arrangement is an insult to floristry, and you should be ashamed of yourself.' Then I hung up.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It turns out the flowers were fine. I was just having a bad day.

jokes.text: Yeah, I was just giving them a little pup talk.
jokes.setup: Alexis catching Ted talking to the puppies
jokes.punchline: Ted's dad joke about 'pep talk' becoming 'pup talk'
jokes.explanation: Classic dad joke territory but perfectly fits Ted's earnest character

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, are you giving the puppies a pep talk?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I was, but I guess you could say it's more of a pup talk.

jokes.text: Oh, I just assumed that's how you always work.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her mind has been elsewhere
jokes.punchline: Ted's innocent observation about her work style
jokes.explanation: Ted's sweet but accidentally insulting observation about Alexis's scattered nature

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: My mind has just been elsewhere lately.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, your mind being elsewhere is kind of your default work style.

jokes.text: Um, but maybe I could take a puppy home for the night. You know I can't let you do that. Yeah, no. That's no problem.
jokes.setup: Alexis's vulnerable confession being rejected
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt to salvage dignity with puppy request
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflection mechanism - Alexis trying to comfort herself after emotional vulnerability

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just... I really like you, and I thought maybe you felt the same way, and I guess I was wrong, so...

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, I do care about you, but I don't think we should—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, no, it's fine. Totally fine. Actually, you know what? Can we just get a puppy?

jokes.text: Um, I was just thinking if there was like, a particularly runty one, like Cindy, or like, no, no. That's okay.
jokes.setup: Alexis still trying to get a puppy after being rejected
jokes.punchline: Her specific request for the saddest puppy before giving up
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking and funny - Alexis identifying with the 'runty' puppy after her own rejection

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Can I just get the saddest puppy you have? Like, the one that looks like it's been through the most? Because honestly, at this point, I feel like we deserve each other.

tmdb_overview: Moira tries to avoid jury duty and David issues a recall on a Rose Apothecary product that causes rashes.
insight: Character escalations drive a solid 77, but sparse punchline moments limit its elite potential.
